body:
## Summary
- Add planner guidance to set `base_pr` when sequential child work follows an open, unmerged child PR.
- Document the sub-orchestrator stacking policy.
- Add a #83-shaped regression for delegating an existing second child with `base_pr: "89"` preserved into the runtime handoff.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

Closes #96

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777761804724 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjM2NDYxODQ6aW1wbGVtZW50Ojk2OnJldmlldzo5Nzoz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR #97 closes #96, which is the focused child of parent #92; the diff addresses the exact #92/#96 request for planner guidance plus a #83-shaped regression. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates both `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` and `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` to document the sequential child stacking policy. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The fix uses the existing structured `base_pr` planner/runtime field and the regression asserts `inputs.base_pr === "89"` rather than encoding stack intent in free-form handoff text. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation relies on existing `delegate_issue` base input plumbing and adds prompt/docs/test coverage instead of introducing new runtime machinery. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The PR touches four targeted files: planner prompt, technical docs, one envelope assertion test, and one focused orchestrator handoff regression. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The change is the smallest policy-level fix requested: explicit planner guidance plus a regression proving the planner/runtime handoff preserves `base_pr`. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are the narrow scope and the use of the existing `base_pr` contract. This matches the requested “small controlled fix” and avoids broadening into the adoption metadata wrinkle.
- The regression covers the important #83 shape: prior child report includes `PR: #89` plus `SHIP`, the planner delegates existing issue #79, and the dispatch payload carries `base_pr: "89"`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. The optional idea to surface the terminal child PR as a structured parent input was intentionally left out, and I would not penalize that under the selected rubrics because the requested controlled fix was prompt guidance plus regression coverage.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #97 addresses the stacking-policy gap raised in #96, which is a focused child of parent issue #92. The underlying problem was that sequential sub-orchestrator child work could continue after a prior child produced an open PR, but the planner was not explicitly told to set `base_pr` for the next child. The PR adds planner guidance, mirrors the behavior in technical docs, and adds a #83-shaped regression proving an existing child handoff preserves `base_pr: "89"`. The optional runtime enhancement to surface the terminal child PR as structured parent input remains out of scope.

## Review
Overall, reviewers agree the PR is narrow, uses the existing `base_pr` contract, and covers the requested behavior at the right boundary. One reviewer identified a minor docs-rendering issue; the other reviews found no actionable concerns.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Docs Paragraph Formatting | WARNING | The new technical-docs sentence is inserted directly after the previous paragraph without a blank line, so Markdown renders it as part of the previous paragraph instead of a distinct policy note. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Codex review found no blocking, warning, or info findings and verified the focused build/test commands.
- The optional structured terminal-child-PR input was intentionally left out of scope and no reviewer treated that as required for this PR.
- I posted one inline comment on `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` line 85 for the formatting issue.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Docs Paragraph Formatting</summary>

**Cause:** The added docs sentence starts immediately after the prior paragraph’s final line, without the blank line CommonMark needs to begin a new paragraph.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a blank line before `When the meta-orchestrator continues sequential child implementation work...`.

**Comments:** This is minor and does not affect runtime behavior or the regression, but it is a concrete docs fix and safe for automation.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the only unresolved finding is a small, concrete branch change that an automated fix-pr pass can safely apply.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add a blank line before the new sequential child stacking paragraph in `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

## Rubrics Review

| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR #97 closes #96, whose body explicitly scopes the child of parent #92 to planner guidance plus a #83-shaped regression; the diff follows that scope. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The PR updates both `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` and `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` with the sequential child stacking policy. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The implementation uses the existing structured `base_pr` field, and the regression asserts the dispatch payload carries `inputs.base_pr === "89"`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR relies on existing `delegate_issue` base input plumbing and adds prompt/docs/test coverage instead of adding new runtime machinery. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to four targeted files: planner prompt, technical docs, one envelope assertion, and one focused handoff regression. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The change implements the smallest controlled fix requested: explicit planner policy plus focused regression coverage for preserving `base_pr`. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are scope control and use of the existing `base_pr` contract. This matches the requested controlled fix and avoids broadening into unrelated adoption metadata behavior.
- The #83-shaped regression covers the key runtime handoff: prior child report includes `PR: #89` plus `SHIP`, planner delegates existing child #79, and the resulting workflow dispatch includes `base_pr: "89"`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. The optional runtime enhancement to surface the terminal child PR as a structured parent input remains out of scope, and the selected rubrics do not require it for this PR.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #97 addresses the sub-orchestrator stacking-policy gap from #96, a focused child of parent issue #92. The issue was that sequential child work could continue after a prior child produced an open PR, but the planner was not explicitly guided to set `base_pr` for the next child. The PR now adds planner guidance, updates technical docs, and includes a #83-shaped regression proving an existing child handoff preserves `base_pr: "89"`. The broader optional idea of surfacing the terminal child PR as structured parent input remains intentionally out of scope.

## Review
The current PR head has no unresolved actionable findings. Claude’s earlier docs-formatting warning was concrete and valid on the original head, but the follow-up fix-pr run added the missing blank line; Rubrics and Codex reviews now find no remaining issues.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | The prior docs paragraph formatting issue has been fixed, and the current diff shows the stacking-policy paragraph separated by blank lines. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Codex review found no blocking, warning, or info findings on PR head `1f36965` and reported build plus focused tests passing.
- The prior inline docs-formatting comment already exists and is resolved by the latest diff; no duplicate inline comment was posted.
- The optional runtime enhancement to surface terminal child PR state remains out of scope and does not block this PR.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved actionable branch-change work remains after the follow-up formatting fix.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #97.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #97 history, linked issues #96 and #92, author associations, repository owner metadata, and the active rubric set. The PR conversation contained only bot/agent-authored review and handoff comments with `author_association: NONE`; the linked human context from `lolipopshock` requested a small controlled fix, not a durable rubric update.

The durable lessons present here are already covered by active rubrics: surgical changes, simple implementation, keep docs in sync, prefer explicit structured inputs, and read linked context first. No rubric files were modified.

title: Guide sequential sub-orchestrator PR stacking
